answer:
The firing angle must be such that the source voltage exceeds the counter emf to allow conduction. The source voltage is 400 sin(θ), and the counter emf is 200 V, so conduction starts when 400 sin(θ) = 200, giving sin(θ) = 0.5, so θ = 30° and 150°. The firing angle range is from 30° to 150°, beyond which the source voltage is always below the counter emf.  

\boxed{F}
